programme.title: Endless Heavyness
programme.desc: Dive into pure power and unrelenting energy. Endless Heavyness is a non-stop wall of sound designed to hit hard and stay loud. No pauses, no compromises—just the most massive tracks to keep the momentum going. It’s not just loud; it’s eternal.

programme.title: Where Heavy Music Lives
programme.desc: Your official gateway to the underground. Discover your first distorted riff or find a circle of peers who "get it." Welcome to the neighborhood—it’s loud, it’s proud, and there’s always room for one more.

programme.title: Metal That Rocks
programme.desc: Where rock 'n' roll swagger meets the weight of metal. This collection bridges the gap, featuring everything from anthemic hard rock to core-shaking classics. It’s loud, it’s proud, and it’s built to move you.

programme.title: Bay Area Godfathers
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Bay Area Godfathers II
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Wishbone Ash - Live in London - 30th Anniversary Concert
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: From The Scene For The Scene
programme.desc: Go beyond the music and into the heart of the underground. This collection pairs heavy-hitting videos with exclusive band interviews and reports from metal capitals worldwide. Built by the scene, for the scene.

programme.title: Finnish Metal Special
programme.desc: From the frozen North comes a sound of haunting melodies and raw intensity. Dive into Finland’s finest, where melancholic atmospheres meet the power of the thousand lakes. It’s a masterclass in darkness and ice-cold riffs.

programme.title: The Home of Heavy
programme.desc: Step into the headquarters for everything that shakes the earth. From the pillars of the classics to the pioneers of the new era, this is where the heart of metal beats loudest. This is where you belong.

programme.title: Night Time Metal
programme.desc: The sun is down, the filters are off, and the real chaos begins. Brace yourself for the raw, uncensored side of the scene too heavy for daylight. This is the unfiltered truth of the underground. Adult ears only.

programme.title: Wishbone Ash - Live in London - 40th Anniversary Concert
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Your Pulse Of Metal
programme.desc: Sync your heartbeat to the complex rhythms of the underground. This collection dives into technical mastery and hidden gems that push your adrenaline. If you’re ready to feel the music in your veins, we’ve got the tracks.

programme.title: For Metalheads Only
programme.desc: Leave the rock anthems at the door—this is 100% pure steel. We’ve dialed the distortion to eleven for a lineup strictly for the heavy-duty fans. No compromises, no fillers, just total sonic devastation.

programme.title: Beker on tour S37
programme.desc: Antipasto carnivoro - Nuove avventure per Fabrizio Nonis, sempre all'insegna della passione di grigliare, in una regione meravigliosa come il Friuli Venezia Giulia, in compagnia di grandi maestri del grill. Tante idee e consigli per cuocere al meglio la carne sul barbec

programme.title: Parola di chef S9
programme.desc: Marina Ravarotto - Il punto di vista di un giovane talento della ristorazione italiana che si racconta svelando le proprie soddisfazioni, difficolta', aspirazioni, in un percorso tra pubblico e privato.

programme.title: Il guusto di Igles
programme.desc: S15 - Le tecniche di lavorazione degli ingredienti e i consigli per utilizzare al meglio tutte le parti della faraona. Le ricette spiegate passo passo per realizzare a casa anche questi piatti.

programme.title: Gambero Rosso
programme.desc: Rubrica

programme.title: Max cucina l'estate
programme.desc: S.2 - Le ricette di Max per l'estate. idee e consigli pratici per piatti veloci, leggeri e gustosi.

programme.title: Giorgione viaggio lungo il Po
programme.desc: Cipolle borettane - A Boretto Giorgione va alla ricerca delle cipolle borettane, la cui coltivazione, dopo un lungo periodo, e' stata riportata nel territorio borettano da alcune piccole aziende agricole

programme.title: Vito con i suoi S12
programme.desc: Apple pay - Vito, Roberto e Lorenzo, 3 generazioni unite non solo da un vincolo di parentela ma dalla comune passione per la cucina. Tra manicaretti e ricette della tradizione uno sguardo divertente e divertito sulla famiglia Bicocchi.

programme.title: Sardegna
programme.desc: Ep3- 1/2 - Giorgione in cammino fra boschi e valli, a piedi, a cavallo o in bici. Le nuove avventure dell'oste piu' amato d'Italia in Sardegna

programme.title: Sardegna
programme.desc: Ep3- 2/2 - Giorgione in cammino fra boschi e valli, a piedi, a cavallo o in bici. Le nuove avventure dell'oste piu' amato d'Italia in Sardegna

programme.title: Del pane non butto via niente
programme.desc: Pane di Matera ep1 - Grandi pani vogliono grandi grani. Il pane di Matera frutto di grani di Puglie e Basilicata, lievito madre, una lenta lievitazione e forno a legna per la cottura. Le ricette di Peppe per utilizzarlo fino all’ultima briciola

programme.title: Mangio tutto tranne Haiti e Repubblica Dominicana
programme.desc: Ile a Vache ep2 - Mare e terra. Pesca e agricoltura a Ile a Vache terra povera ma ricca di natura, Giovanni incontra Luca Gargano che ha fatto di Haiti la sua seconda casa.

programme.title: Parola di chef S9
programme.desc: Antonio Greco - Il punto di vista di un giovane talento della ristorazione italiana che si racconta svelando le proprie soddisfazioni, difficolta', aspirazioni, in un percorso tra pubblico e privato.

programme.title: Beker on tour S37
programme.desc: Beef ribs - Nuove avventure per Fabrizio Nonis, sempre all'insegna della passione di grigliare, in una regione meravigliosa come il Friuli Venezia Giulia, in compagnia di grandi maestri del grill. Tante idee e consigli per cuocere al meglio la carne sul barbecue

programme.title: Come è profondo il mare le Isole Pontine
programme.desc: Palmarola - Palmarola, un’isola incantata, dai fondali meravigliosi. Isola selvaggia e quasi disabitata. L’incontro in mare con un pescatore darà a Gianfranco la materia prima per la sua ricetta.

programme.title: Gambero Rosso
programme.desc: Rubrica

programme.title: Il guusto di Igles
programme.desc: S15 - Un crostaceo molto saporito, le canocchie nella cucina di Igles ben si associano ad un piatto di spaghetti. I consigli di Igles per un buon sugo di canocchie e uno sformato di canocchie

programme.title: Max alla brace
programme.desc: S.1 - Max Mariola in cortile alle prese con un bbq. Ricette gustose e originali da realizzare nel giardino di casa o per passare qualche ora all’aperto

programme.title: Giorgione viaggio lungo il Po
programme.desc: Aceto balsamico - Il viaggio prosegue verso Boretto dove Giorgione visita l’Acetaia San Giacomo, tempio dell’Aceto Balsamico. Qui apprende che l’ingrediente più importante per l’aceto balsamico e' il tempo.

programme.title: Peppe Guida e i suoi fratelli
programme.desc: Pollo arrosto, Risotto asparagi, gnocchi della nonna - Peppe Guida riunisce tutti a Montechiaro, fratelli, sorelle e mamma. Una famiglia legata dalla comune passione per la cucina e le ricette della tradizione.

programme.title: Sardegna
programme.desc: Ep4 - 1/2 Giorgione in cammino fra boschi e valli, a piedi, a cavallo o in bici. Le nuove avventure dell'oste piu' amato d'Italia in Sardegna

programme.title: Sardegna
programme.desc: Ep4 - 2/2 Giorgione in cammino fra boschi e valli, a piedi, a cavallo o in bici. Le nuove avventure dell'oste piu' amato d'Italia in Sardegna

programme.title: Le grandi cantine S2
programme.desc: Buttafuoco - Un tour nelle più importanti famiglie di viticultori italiani. Uomini che hanno creduto in un sogno e lo hanno trasformato in realtà e che hanno dedicato la vita al lavoro in vigna. Storie di vecchie e nuove generazioni di vignaioli.

programme.title: Come è profondo il mare le Isole Pontine
programme.desc: Palmarola - Palmarola, un’isola incantata, dai fondali meravigliosi. Isola selvaggia e quasi disabitata. L’incontro in mare con un pescatore darà a Gianfranco la materia prima per la sua ricetta.

programme.title: Puglia food stories
programme.desc: Da Antonio, uno dei maestri del panzerotto, Domingo rivive i momenti della sua infanzia, quando con un panzerotto bollente in mano passeggiava tra i vicoli in compagnia di sua madre.

programme.title: Beker on tour S37
programme.desc: Antipasto carnivoro - Nuove avventure per Fabrizio Nonis, sempre all'insegna della passione di grigliare, in una regione meravigliosa come il Friuli Venezia Giulia, in compagnia di grandi maestri del grill. Tante idee e consigli per cuocere al meglio la carne sul barbec

programme.title: Parola di chef S9
programme.desc: Marina Ravarotto - Il punto di vista di un giovane talento della ristorazione italiana che si racconta svelando le proprie soddisfazioni, difficolta', aspirazioni, in un percorso tra pubblico e privato.

programme.title: Mangio tutto tranne Haiti e Repubblica Dominicana
programme.desc: Ile a Vache ep2 - Mare e terra. Pesca e agricoltura a Ile a Vache terra povera ma ricca di natura, Giovanni incontra Luca Gargano che ha fatto di Haiti la sua seconda casa.

programme.title: Il guusto di Igles
programme.desc: S15 - Le tecniche di lavorazione degli ingredienti e i consigli per utilizzare al meglio tutte le parti della faraona. Le ricette spiegate passo passo per realizzare a casa anche questi piatti.

programme.title: Max cucina l'estate
programme.desc: S.2 - Le ricette di Max per l'estate. idee e consigli pratici per piatti veloci, leggeri e gustosi.

programme.title: Giorgione viaggio lungo il Po
programme.desc: Cipolle borettane - A Boretto Giorgione va alla ricerca delle cipolle borettane, la cui coltivazione, dopo un lungo periodo, e' stata riportata nel territorio borettano da alcune piccole aziende agricole

programme.title: Vito con i suoi S12
programme.desc: Gnocchi di barba e risotto con folaga - Vito, Roberto e Lorenzo, 3 generazioni unite non solo da un vincolo di parentela ma dalla comune passione per la cucina. Tra manicaretti e ricette della tradizione uno sguardo divertente e divertito sulla famiglia Bicocchi.

programme.title: Sardegna
programme.desc: Ep4 - 1/2 Giorgione in cammino fra boschi e valli, a piedi, a cavallo o in bici. Le nuove avventure dell'oste piu' amato d'Italia in Sardegna

programme.title: Sardegna
programme.desc: Ep4 - 2/2 Giorgione in cammino fra boschi e valli, a piedi, a cavallo o in bici. Le nuove avventure dell'oste piu' amato d'Italia in Sardegna

programme.title: Ritratto di chef
programme.desc: S2 - La vita, la carriera, la famiglia. i sogni e i sacrifici raccontati dai protagonisti. Uomini e donne che hanno dedicato la loro vita al cibo e alla sua interpretazione

programme.title: Le grandi famiglie del vino
programme.desc: Puiatti - Identità, ricerca e sfida sono gli ingredienti di questa storia. Nata negli anni 60 Puiatti rappresenta una visione unica e particolare di questo territorio.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef
programme.desc: Join our host, Chef Natalia Machado as she introduces us to her favourite techniques for using a wide variety of versatile, savoury tomatoes.

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from another award-winning cookbook: Eat Alberta First by Karen Anderson! Follow along as Shahir prepares lamb shanks with saskatoon berries, lip smacking good ribs, fish in mushroom cream sauce, and much more!

programme.title: Cook the Books
programme.desc: Chef-host Shahir Massoud tries out recipes from another award-winning cookbook: Craving Vegan by Sam Turnbull! Follow along as Shahir prepares vegan white lasagna, jackfruit pulled pork, tofu tikka masala, and much more!

programme.title: Blitz!
programme.desc: BLITZ! is an all-new cooking show! Imagine being next Erica Karbelnik as they cook their fav dishes!

programme.title: Blitz!
programme.desc: BLITZ! is an all-new cooking show! Imagine being next Josh Karbelnik as they cook their fav dishes!

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: A symbol of comfort and city pride, the Philly cheesesteak continues to inspire fresh imagination. Thin-sliced steak, melting cheese, and griddled bread set the foundation as new flavors and regional twists bring this iconic sandwich into a new era of creativity.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Game day traditions meet sizzling creativity as familiar favorites hit the griddle. From sliders to smoky wings, these dishes celebrate casual gatherings and the inventive ways home chefs turn everyday ingredients into shareable, crowd-pleasing bites.

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Around the world in one meal, global flavors come together on the griddle. Vibrant spices, sauces, and ingredients mix to form creative fusions that celebrate connection through food, offering a taste of adventure in every sizzling, flavorful bite.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Simple, fun, and magically tasty, some of Vietnam's most delicious and well-known foods come from its capital city of Hanoi. Vietnamese expert Lisa Nguyen shares her knowledge of Hanoi dishes by starting off with a sweet, sour, and spicy papaya salad called Goi Du Du. With a popular nuoc cham dressing featuring sugar, lime juice and Thai chili drizzled over a medley of crunchy carrots, fresh cilantro and mint, chewy papaya, and shrimp, this dish is the perfect balance of wonderful flavours and textures. Also on the menu, grilled-to-perfection Pork Patties with a rich caramel sauce. Fresh fragrant dill and golden turmeric elevate Lisa's next dish as she cooks up some mahi-mahi to enjoy...

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Nuts are a staple in Lebanon - they add depth of flavour and make dishes more hearty. Talented home cook Jasmin Ibrahim shows us a unique Lebanese dish that is traditionally made in the dead of winter - Winter Tabbouleh. With no fresh herbs or vegetables, this crunchy nut, feta, and tasty bulgur salad will get you through a long winter night. Next, she makes a simmering beef tortellini with toasted pine nuts in a hot, tangy yoghurt sauce. She then cooks up tender Celebration Lamb showered in three kinds of tasty nuts; it's great for sharing at special occasions. To finish off, Jasmin prepares some delicious walnut-filled Lebanese Pancakes doused in sweet rose water - yum! Ditch the...

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Greek expert Joanna Chery showcases unique recipes from the island of Crete. To start, she whips up a creamy Cretan Meat Pie. Sweet ricotta, tender lamb, and an aromatic bundle of spices covered in a flaky Greek yoghurt pie dough might be the best Greek comfort food. Next, Joanna makes Grouper with Okra - a light and healthy dish featuring flaky sea bass, fresh okra, and delicious potatoes. Then, she prepares a dish that's traditionally served at weddings - Gamopilafo. It's a fluffy, citrusy, rice pilaf topped with crispy, juicy chicken that will have you going back for seconds! For dessert, a popular Cretan treat called Baklava. Joanna expertly layers crispy, golden-brown phyllo pastry...

programme.title: Holiday Eatz
programme.desc: Our fun and occasionally silly chef-host Kimberly Lallouz is taking on a Game Day menu for whatever bowl, cup, or ring your favourite team is bringing home this year! Wear your team colors and cook along as she updates perennial favourites like Chicken Wings (...grilled and smothered in savoury Suya spice!) and Loaded Nachos (with blue corn chips and decadently vegan with her chipotle-cashew 'cheeze!') Next, her Philly-style cheesy flank steak hoagie is a sure crowd pleaser, and for a final play, it's back to the grill with shrimp skewer made Buffalo-style with hot sauce, butter, and creamy avocado ranch dressing. Game on!

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Talented baker Jessica McGovern shows us how to get fancy with three stunning French desserts.

programme.title: Holiday Eatz
programme.desc: Summer Solstice is celebrated around the world with all manner of ritual and fun"... Holiday Eatz host Kimberly Lallouz is celebrating with a party menu that takes advantage of all those extra hours of sun! Homemade cheese in one day? You bet! It's easy and delicious in her Watermelon Salad with white balsamic vinegar and sunflower seed brittle. Her next course is grilled shrimp, scallops, avocados, and peaches? Yep! Washed down with a citrus spiked white sangria! A crispy skin pork belly Porchetta roast is next, stuffed with savoury summer herbs. For her finale, you'll love her rhubarb meringue pie, it's a mile high pie for a mile-wide smile"... what a way to spend the longest day!

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern gives us a sweet taste of chocolate heaven with an overload of chocolatey desserts including a decadent Bombe au Chocolat.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: In this "C" challenge, Chef Robert Jewell cooks up recipes using coconut and crab before combining both into a single recipe. Using sweet coconut, Robert makes a classic dessert -- an irresistible coconut cream pie. He starts with a delectable crunchy graham cracker crust and tops his pie with fluffy whipped cream and toasted coconut flakes. Next, Robert transforms a fresh soft-shell crab into a delicious, spicy, and crunchy dish. The best part? It's fast and easy to make. After soaking the crab in milk, Robert dunks it into a flour-cornmeal mixture and fries it until golden brown. But that's not all, Robert whips up the perfect dipping sauce, using spicy smoked- jalapeno, lime, and...

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: It's all about sesame and scallops in this letter "S" challenge, and host Theresa Visintin has some appetizing ideas up her sleeve. First, she shows us how to make scallop cassoulet, a simple recipe that can be cooked even faster than it can eaten! Theresa browns the tender scallops in delicious bacon fat and then bakes it on a bed of creamy kidney beans. Next, Theresa uses sesame seeds to create a zesty sauce for a take-out favourite, made in your very own kitchen - noodles! For her final recipe, our culinary host combines sesame and scallops, creating a juicy, crunchy masterpiece.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Making dishes with the letter “W” could be seen as a real challenge, but Chef Lauren Gulyas has got it covered. Choosing water chestnut and watercress as her star ingredients, Chef Lauren begins by serving up an Asian inspired rice dish complete with crunchy water chestnuts. She starts off cooking rice in chicken broth, a great way to infuse the grain with a subtle savoury flavour. Sautéing celery, garlic, and onion in sesame oil adds a flavour kick. Throw in some ginger, veggies, and water chestnuts and enjoy the sound of the sizzle. Next up, Lauren puts a peppery twist on a classic recipe, linguine with watercress pesto. Then it’s time for the grand finale, Chef Lauren uses both...

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Tea and turkey are an unlikely combo for a meal, but Chef Robert Jewell is a creative force in the kitchen. First, he gives earl grey and lavender tea a sweet treatment by making a rich semifreddo. Robert creates a silky tea infused syrup, which he drizzles over a luxurious custard and tops with whipped cream. Robert punches up the colour by adding tart poached raspberries and vibrant violet lavender petals. Now, it's time for a real show stopper - saffron turkey kebabs with pomegranate rice. This is not your average turkey dinner. First, Robert marinates the turkey pieces in lime, yogourt and garlic. After infusing basmati rice with aromatic saffron and bay leaves, Robert tosses the rice...

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: For a quick and tasty meal, chef Spencer Watts cooks up fast favs using scallops and trout, all done in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Fish the Dish
programme.desc: Chef and seafood lover Spencer Watts mixes the sweet flavours of fruit with fish as he makes dishes with salmon and scallops.

programme.title: Global Street Eats
programme.desc: Chef Dev prepares some of his favorite street foods that feature coconut, including Coconut Roti Flatbreads from Sri Lanka, God’s Bread from Portugal, and Mango Coconut Sticky Rice from Thailand.

programme.title: Global Street Eats
programme.desc: Chef Dev shares recipes for some of his favorite street food desserts from around the world, including Custard Tarts from Portugal, Alfajores Sandwich Cookies from Argentina, and Purple Corn Pudding from Peru.

programme.title: Global Street Eats
programme.desc: Chef Dev prepares comforting street food dishes from around the world, including Bindaetteok Pancakes from South Korea, Chicken Tamales from Mexico, and Sausage Rolls from the UK.

programme.title: Global Street Eats
programme.desc: Chef Dev shows off street food dishes from around the world that feature basil, including Pesto Rice Balls from Italy, Holy Basil Chicken from Thailand, and Green Papaya and Beef Jerky Salad with Thai Basil from Vietnam.

programme.title: Everyday Excellent
programme.desc: With only 30 minutes and the clock ticking, can Vanessa make an allergen friendly meal for 4 for about 40 bucks? On the menu: kale and lentil salad with green goddess dressing, chickpea curry, and avocado key lime pie cups!

programme.title: Everyday Excellent
programme.desc: With only 30 minutes and the clock ticking, can Vanessa make breakfast for dinner for 4 for about 40 bucks? On the menu: a veggie frittata, sweet and peppery bacon, a cottage cheese pancake stack, and fruit skewers!

programme.title: Holiday Eatz
programme.desc: Our fun and occasionally silly chef-host Kimberly Lallouz is taking on a Game Day menu for whatever bowl, cup, or ring your favourite team is bringing home this year! Wear your team colors and cook along as she updates perennial favourites like Chicken Wings (...grilled and smothered in savoury Suya spice!) and Loaded Nachos (with blue corn chips and decadently vegan with her chipotle-cashew 'cheeze!') Next, her Philly-style cheesy flank steak hoagie is a sure crowd pleaser, and for a final play, it's back to the grill with shrimp skewer made Buffalo-style with hot sauce, butter, and creamy avocado ranch dressing. Game on!

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: A symbol of comfort and city pride, the Philly cheesesteak continues to inspire fresh imagination. Thin-sliced steak, melting cheese, and griddled bread set the foundation as new flavors and regional twists bring this iconic sandwich into a new era of creativity.

programme.title: Holiday Eatz
programme.desc: Summer Solstice is celebrated around the world with all manner of ritual and fun"... Holiday Eatz host Kimberly Lallouz is celebrating with a party menu that takes advantage of all those extra hours of sun! Homemade cheese in one day? You bet! It's easy and delicious in her Watermelon Salad with white balsamic vinegar and sunflower seed brittle. Her next course is grilled shrimp, scallops, avocados, and peaches? Yep! Washed down with a citrus spiked white sangria! A crispy skin pork belly Porchetta roast is next, stuffed with savoury summer herbs. For her finale, you'll love her rhubarb meringue pie, it's a mile high pie for a mile-wide smile"... what a way to spend the longest day!

programme.title: Griddle Nation
programme.desc: Around the world in one meal, global flavors come together on the griddle. Vibrant spices, sauces, and ingredients mix to form creative fusions that celebrate connection through food, offering a taste of adventure in every sizzling, flavorful bite.

programme.title: Cheeseburgers of the World
programme.desc: Chef Joe Friday cooks up a Maine Lobster Burger from the USA, Rice Burger from Japan, and Chimichurri Burger from the Dominican Republic.

programme.title: Corktales
programme.desc: Pull up a stool and join master mixologist Yvonne Langen as she shows us how to prepare The Last Word, a gin fizz, a Moscow mule, and more on Corktales!

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Grill master Spencer Watts shows us four ways to barbeque a classic. Succulent, tender, mouth-watering ribs.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Barbecuing bananas, that's crazy. Chef Spencer Watts shows us how to grill the delicate, evERS-scrumptious fruit. Bananas.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Fish. Flaky, flavoursome, and perfect for barbeque. High energy host Spencer Watts tests out his grill with delightfully tender fish dishes.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Shellfish is delicate, tantalizing, and full of flavour, especially when it's grilled to perfection

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Barbeque is all about sauces, rubs and marinades

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Salsa means undeniable flavour! Lively host Spencer Watts plays with sweet, salty, and spicy in four amazing salsa recipes.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Grilling expert Spencer Watts hosts a tasty beef-a-palooza with four incredible beef cuts including smoked brisket and succulent beef tenderloin.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Barbeque is not just about meat! Chef Spencer Watts shows us how to grill fruits and vegetables like a pro.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Charismatic host Spencer Watts shows us why chicken is best on the grill with four irresistible chicken recipes.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: There are limitless options with lamb on the grill! Host Spencer Watts offers some tips and tricks on how to prepare four different cuts of lamb.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Whether for a small family dinner or a big backyard barbeque, host Spencer Watts shows us the best ways to grill up tasty duck, turkey, capon, and quail.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Never underestimate the power of a good side or dessert! Fun host Spencer Watts grills up the perfect sides and treats for your next barbeque.

programme.title: Holiday Eatz
programme.desc: Summer Solstice is celebrated around the world with all manner of ritual and fun"... Holiday Eatz host Kimberly Lallouz is celebrating with a party menu that takes advantage of all those extra hours of sun! Homemade cheese in one day? You bet! It's easy and delicious in her Watermelon Salad with white balsamic vinegar and sunflower seed brittle. Her next course is grilled shrimp, scallops, avocados, and peaches? Yep! Washed down with a citrus spiked white sangria! A crispy skin pork belly Porchetta roast is next, stuffed with savoury summer herbs. For her finale, you'll love her rhubarb meringue pie, it's a mile high pie for a mile-wide smile"... what a way to spend the longest day!

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Onions, green onions, chives, garlic, and leeks are all members of the same fabulous flowering family, Allium! First up? Chef Watts shows us his sweet and smoky Charred Leeks with Romesco; add grilled toast and you have your next party appetizer! Next up is a main course. Spencer starts with a juicy grilled pork chop, then adds roasted garlic melted into a balsamic onion chutney to make his out-of-this-world Roasted Garlic Pork Chops. Looking for a crowd pleaser that's easy to make? Spencer has a recipe for that! Chicken Yakitori. He threads skewers with crunchy green onions and tender chicken and caramelizes the yakitori sauce as he grills this tasty dish. Are you ready to drool? Spencer...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Have a cheese lover coming over? Blow their minds with some of Spencer's "grate" barbecued cheese recipes! First up, Spencer chars Brie Cheese to an ooey-gooey perfection and tops it with tart cherries and crunchy walnuts, serving it with crispy grilled crostinis. Next up is a Grilled Steak and Caesar with a twist only Spencer could add - grilled... lettuce! Then, Spencer uses the classic German "hasselback" presentation to stuff chicken breasts with verdant spinach, yummy Swiss cheese and pungent sun- dried tomatoes, grilling them to melty perfection. Finally, Spencer fires up his barbecue to make his Grilled Peaches with Salted Bourbon Caramel, a decadent dessert elevated with creamy...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: In this bacon loving episode, Chef Spencer delivers a tasty Bacon Weave Turkey Breast, which gets injected with beer and spices before being wrapped up in its cozy bacon blanket and sent off to the grill! Next up? A Peameal Bacon Roast with Rosti. This heavy hunk of back bacon gets smoked to flavourful perfection while Chef Watts makes the crispiest golden-fried-to-perfection potato pancake. Then, Spencer whips up a charry, grilled, super decadent, bacon-wrapped and bacon stuffed cheeseburger. Spencer isn't finished with bacon yet! For his final recipe, Spencer stuffs an apple with decadent crispy morsels stuffed along with savoury caramelized onions and melty cheddar cheese.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: This episode kicks off with Spencer's tasty Grilled Caesar Salad. He skillfully piles of char-grilled romaine lettuce wedges and sourdough croutons are topped with tangy dressing, crispy pancetta, and shards of a homemade parmesan tuile. For his next recipe, Spencer whips up Grilled Zucchini Boats that filled with fresh ingredients including orzo pasta, summer veggies, and herbs. Chef Watts loves a good steak and his Grilled Cauliflower Steak is not one to be passed up! Dusted in Middle Eastern Spices, flame roasted and served with chili-spiked, Yemeni 'Skhug' hot sauce and pomegranate seeds. Finally, Spencer steams tender artichokes, stuffing these flowers with breadcrumbs and...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: The most liked post on any grill-master's social feed is 'meat on a stick' - and Chef Spencer's Turkish-style chicken kabob is worth every click! He marinates thick juicy chicken morsels in yogurt and spices, and then grills these flavourful skewers to a charry perfection. Next up is the creative 'Tornado Potato' which re- invents grilled potatoes with a wild presentation and a trio of toppings. Spencer then assembles Char Siu Sliders on tiny party- ready buns. Stuffed with a soy, honey and umami-packed hoisin lacquered slow-and-low grilled pork belly, these tasty little sliders will have your taste buds tingling. For his final recipe, Spencer whips up toasted crispy roti cups; topping...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer starts this spicy barbeque episode with flavours from Thailand with a Bok Choy with a Ginger Chili Sauce. Powerful bird's eye chilies are packed into a sweet and spicy sauce and served over tasty grilled bok choy. Spencer then introduces us to Calabrian chilies, mashed into a spice paste and rubbed on butterflied chicken that then gets grilled to flame-kissed perfection. Next, Spencer gives fresh calamari some heat with Korean chilies that add their distinctive heat and flavour before grilling this scrumptious recipe and serving it over a comforting bed of rice and peas. Finally, Spencer prepares Seekh Kabobs, made with ground lamb spiked with punchy dried Kashmiri chilies...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: First stop on Chef Spencer's Spanish flavour tour? Habanero Char-Grilled Octopus - warmed up with punchy chilies and balanced with a sweet and tangy sherry vinaigrette. Next, Spencer stuffs whole sea bass with citrus and herbs, gives it the grill treatment and then tops it with olive oil blistered cherry tomatoes. The rotisserie does the heavy lifting for Mr. Watts' whole roast chicken - spiked with bright Spanish flavours like Spanish chilies and smoked paprika, slow cooked and served extra juicy. Finally, Spencer prepares a classic Spanish recipe: Paella. Loaded saffron rice, piled high with chorizo, veggies, and shellfish. Of course, Spencer's Paella is made in the Spanish tradition;...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: It's all about beautiful butter in this episode of Watts on the Grill! First up, entertaining host Spencer fires tosses clams in velvety butter, zesty garlic and earthy spices to make his Clams with Garlic Paprika Butter. Next up is a traditional Indian recipe, but done on the grill - Butter Chicken! All the butter chicken flavours we love are infused in the marinade, before getting crisped up on the fiery grill - the result is both classic and completely new! Scallops are next, grilled and served with a mouth-watering caper-raisin butter. For his final recipe; Chef Watts prepares a rich and juicy grilled Rib Steak topped with a decadent port wine compound butter. Oh, what a butter-loving...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: We've all cooked with salt. But what about ON salt. That's right, chef Spencer Watts blows our minds with his seared scallops that are cooked on a Himalayan pink salt block. His next recipe uses a quick cure with Hawaiian black lava salt on swordfish steaks, then tops them with a gorgeous and refreshing grilled fruit salsa. Then, Spencer gets creative with Cured Duck Legs with Scallions, using a salt cure to intensify the flavours of duck leg before it's cooked slow and low, then glazed with maple and soy. After cooking on salt, curing with salt, the only thing left is to cook INSIDE salt - Spencer's Salt Crusted Prime Rib is a game changer, flavourful, bone-in beef that he seasons with a...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Chocolate on the barbecue? That's right. Chef Spencer Watts kicks off this episode as he grills a crispy coconut-cacao crust and serves it with sweet chili dipping sauce. Chocolate is the not-so-secret ingredient in Spencer's Grilled Chicken Molé. He grills the chicken legs smothered in the sweet-spicy-salty-nutty-creamy-chocolatey sauce, then finishes it by cooking it slow and low. Next, dark chocolate cocoa powder is in both the rub and the sauce for Chef Watts' favourite pork rib recipe - Cocoa-rubbed Baby Back Ribs. For his final recipe, Spencer takes it to a sweet level with his Chocolate pizza made with chocolate ganache, grilled cherries, with barbeque- caramelized white chocolate...

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: It's time to celebrate the art of smoking with wood! You'll drool over Spencer's Cherrywood Smoked Chicken Wings, dry rubbed with his favourite spices and smoked with cherrywood. Next, Spencer marinates sable fish in Japanese ingredients and grills it on a sweet-smoky cedar plank. Then, Spencer shows off his Texas BBQ skills with his Mesquite Smoked Skirt Steak. Engulfed in a thick cloud of white smoke, this skirt steak has the flavour and texture of tasty brisket, but made in a fraction amount of time. Finally, Spencer gives a pork shoulder a long, smoky cook in oak wood. The wait is worth it as he serves it piled high on a bun and topped with a mustardy Carolina BBQ sauce.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and his team visit the tennis club and serve up some grilled eats for the club members. Smoked Salmon, Beef Tenderloin with Cherry Sauce, Asparagus with Citrus Butter with a Grand Slam Beet Salad are the perfect match after a long day on the courts

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and the Team prepare BBQ Sunday Dinner with ingredients from the Farmers Market. Herbed Prime Rib, Grilled Pork Chops with Garlic and Herbs, Roasted Root Veggies with Mustard Glaze, and Yorkshire pudding will please friends and family alike!

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Using internationally inspired recipes, Matt and the Road Grill team take us on a taste bud tour around the world with Stuffed Peppers, Bison Burgers with Oka Cheese, Tandoori Lamb Ribs and Tea Smoked Hoisin Duck.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and the Team cook up a mound of food for hungry athletes raising money for charity. No one went hungry with Beef Dip Sandwiches; Lemon Honey Garlic Baby Back Pork Ribs; and fresh Grilled Mahi-mahi with sweet Pineapple Tomatillo Salsa.

programme.title: Holiday Eatz

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: The Buskers Festival is in town! The team hit the grill to do some BBQ entertaining. They cook up a feast for the crowd including a Roast of Pork with Concord Grape Sauce, Spicy Shrimp, and Beer Braised Sausage with Caramelized Onions

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: The Road Grill Team go to a neighbourhood Garage Sale, then treat everyone to everyday dishes with a twist. Matt and his team grill up some Grilled Porterhouse Steaks in a Homemade Steak Spice; Grilled Lemon Prosciutto Scallops; Planked Stuffed Potatoes and a Grilled Caesar Salad.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt loves animals - including dogs. He goes to a Dog Show to give dog lovers a little taste of what the QB is cooking - Garlicky Grilled Artichokes; Bacon Wrapped Halibut With Lime Scallion Sauce Sauce; Slow Cooked Spareribs With Guava Glaze; and A Summer Wild Berry Crisp.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and the Team grill up some chow at a bike ride Fundraiser. They tempt the crowd with Hazelnut Crusted Halibut with Strawberry Salsa, Pork Picnic Roast with Blackberry Chipotle Glaze, Grilled Paninis with Roast Veggies, and Spinach Salad with Apples, Bacon and Cashews

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and his team hit fitness central and cook up a grilled feast that is high on protein and lean on fat including Salt Crusted Peel and Eat Shrimp; Lime Chipotle Pork Medallions; Mesquite Smoked Turkey with Peach Glaze and a Sweet and Spicy Black Bean Salad.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and the Road Grill team are pitching in to feed volunteers some great grilled chow including Lime Jalapeno Chicken Wings with Raita Dip; Beef Ribs with Vinegar Bbq Sauce; Killer Bbq Fries with Homemade Ketchup and Some Ice Cold Kiwi Melon Frostys

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and the Team join some friends at a Cottage Party to celebrate the end of school. They keep the festivities going with Bbqed Rib Eye Steaks with a Chimmi Churri Sauce; Jerk Pulled Pork Sandwiches with Fresh Mango; Roasted Garlic Onion Potatoes and Greek Salad in a Pita.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Not only do the racers in the Soap Box Derby have a need for speed, they have a need for BBQ! Matt and the RG team cook up a feast to slow down any speedster. The menu: Smoked Shrimp B.L.T., Pineapple Basil Rotisserie Chicken, Grilled Sweet Potato Pops, and Summer Berry Pudding.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt spends the day by the water; fishing, playing Frisbee, catching some rays. What goes with water and sun? BBQ!!! Matt & his team make a seafood buffet of Grilled Clams in the Shell with Serrano Ham, Smoked Lake Rainbow Trout, Grilled Lobster with Grapefruit Salsa, and Crab-Stuffed Avocados.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt gets down and dirty playing some beach Volleyball. In between bumps, sets, and spikes, the RG team serves up a smorgasbord of great food. The menu: Grilled Chicken Breast on Foccacia with Grilled Apples & Cambozola Cheese, Sezchuan Ribs with Chunky Plum Dipping Sauce, Roasted Corn and Pepper Potato Salad, and Homemade Protein Bars

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Forget bicycle built for two. This Big Bike fits 30 people! Matt & the team join forces with the Heart & Stroke foundation to raise money by riding around a track. Matt feeds these 'bikers' some healthy and delicious grub. The menu: Grilled Calamari, Smoked Turkey Fajitas, Grilled Scallops and Watermelon Salsa, and Cold Gazpacho.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: It's a bird, it's a plane, it's a day of great BBQ. Inspired by the aerial acrobatics of CF18's and Harvards, Matt cooks up a variety of poultry and fowl. The menu: Bacon Wrapped Quail Stuffed with 3 Cream Cheeses, Raspberry Chipotle Turkey Wings, Grilled Balsamic Marinated Duck Breast, and Deviled Eggs.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Whether you're a pro or a beginner, everyone is welcome to join the Road Grill team at the 19th tee where Matt is busy burnin' up his Q's. What better way to end your day at the links with an enticing menu of Miso Citrus Grilled Sablefish, Smoked Rack of Pork with a Sour Cherry Sauce , Lemon Basil Smashed Potatoes with Mushrooms, Arugula Salad with Peaches, and Blue Cheese and Walnuts.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: In between hoisting sails and scrubbing the deck, Matt and his team prepare a feast for the sailors before going on a long voyage. Not only is the weather perfect for sailing, it's perfect for BBQing! The menu: Scallop Corn Bacon Burger, Maple-apple Brined Pork Chops, Grilled Tuna Bruschetta, and Strawberry Marscapone Trifle.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Teddy Bears of all age, shape, and size join our grill team with their owners for a day of games, rides and some teddy bear repair. Matt & the RG team are BBQing a menu even the pickiest eater will love. The menu: Grilled Almond Butter & Banana Sandwiches, Grilled Meatball Sub, Smoked White Cheddar Macaroni, and Quick & Snappy Ginger Lemon Ice Cream Sandwiches.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Llamas, horses, and pigs...Oh My! The RG team lends it support to a farm animal rescue. Matt sets up his grills during a weekend fundraiser and stays friendly to his animal buddies by serving up a vegetarian menu. The menu: Portobello Burger Stack, Smoked Corn Tamale with Goat Cheese& Salsa Fresca, Pizza Salad Sandwich, and Tabbouleh Salad

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: The neighbourhood outdoor pool is a great way to cool down on a hot summer day. Matt and the Grill Team are dipping their feet in the pool, splashing with the kids, and, of course, grilling up some fabulous eats to munch on while playing in the park or relaxing on your towel. The menu: BBQ'd Pork Tenderloin & Pears on Bagels, Grilled Asian Chicken Burger, Surf & Turf Kabobs with Arugula Pesto, and BBQ'd Cinnamon Donuts.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: It's graduation day at Chef School and Matt and the Grill team are sharing in the festivities. To congratulate the new Chefs, Matt has cooked up a masterful menu of fancy, yet simple, dishes. The menu: Mixed Seafood Grill on a Plank, Maple Rosemary Rotisserie Chicken, Dunked Hunan Lamb Rack, and Salmon Pastrami.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: The Road Grill team goes rural and gets lost in hedge mazes at Saunders Farm. Luckily, they find their way out in time to cook up some BBQ and feed all families at the farm. The menu: Grilled Chicken Legs with Crunchy Chip Crust, Smoked Ham with Maple Mustard Glaze, Planked Asparagus Bundles with Cheese and Prosciutto, and Cobb Salad.

programme.desc: La misión es llevar el mensaje de libertad a las naciones. A través de ganar almas y hacer discípulos podemos ayudar a las naciones a hacer una vida de libertad

programme.title: Jentezen Franklin
programme.desc: Encuentre respuestas en la Palabra de Dios y aprenda a vivir cada día con un propósito mientras Jentezen Franklin presenta la verdad clara que lo inspirará.

programme.title: Impactando las Naciones
programme.desc: En Impactando Naciones, la Pastora Wanda Rolón comparte mensajes de fe, esperanza y poder espiritual para transformar vidas, familias y comunidades.

programme.title: El Programa de John Ankerberg
programme.desc: Acompañe a John Ankerberg en debates espirituales profundos y obtenga respuestas bíblicas a preguntas difíciles.

programme.title: En Familia con Luis y Kristen
programme.desc: Todo padre ha sido llamado a ser el protector de su hogar y cuando él falta en la casa hay un hueco enorme que solo Dios puede llenar.

programme.title: Andrés Spyker de Más Vida
programme.desc: Conéctate cada semana con un mensaje fresco de la palabra de Dios con una aplicación práctica para tu vida.

In this video, she explores Morocco’s most authentic city and discovers where Europe meets Africa in a fascinating blend of culture, history, and architecture. It’s not overrun with tourists, it’s 100% real, and it ended up being her favorite city in Morocco!

programme.title: Whats On Music Festivals All Over the Med
programme.desc: The Mediterranean comes alive to the sound of music. From Malta high energy Rock the South and sophisticated Malta Jazz Festival, to Italy cutting edge Limbo Festival, Spain forward thinking Sonar Festival, and Turkey classical and contemporary Istanbul Music Festival, every corner of the region vibrates with rhythm, passion, and creativity.

programme.title: Med Nations 15 Montenegro

programme.title: Madame Butterfly Teatro Alla Scala 2016
programme.desc: In Madama Butterfly (Teatro alla Scala, 2016), a young Japanese woman enters into a marriage with an American naval officer, filled with hope for a new life. programme.title: Whats On Mediterranean Special Events
programme.desc: From Malta to Morocco, unique festivals capture Mediterranean creativity. Malta hosts the Valletta Green Festival, Notte Bianca, Malta Air Show, and Gharb International Kite and Wind Festival. Portugal celebrates with Festa das Cruzes, Morocco Dream City Festival dazzles with arts and music, Croatia brings the avant garde MoDem Festival, and Italy hosts the historic Venice Debutante Grand Ball. These festivals showcase the extraordinary, the festive, and the unforgettable.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Kaštela (U masliniku)
programme.desc: In Kaštela, a cluster of seven towns near Split, winemaking runs deep. Among olive groves and vineyards, Mladen Kuzmanić treated us to local flavors—sipa i bob, pancetta, goat cheese, and homemade sausage—perfectly paired with a glass of regional wine.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Sevilla
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Seville, Spain — a vibrant city in the heart of Andalusia known for its rich Moorish heritage, flamenco music, and stunning architecture. She visits iconic landmarks like the Seville Cathedral, Plaza de España, and the Royal Alcázar, while also discovering local tapas bars and lively streets filled with culture and charm.

programme.title: MEMOIRS JUNE 20
programme.desc: MEMOIRS JUNE 20

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. Imagine the sun setting over a lake in Tuscany as the Puccini Festival opens its 72nd season with the powerful melodies of Turandot. From that timeless elegance, we head straight into the high energy of Malta, where the Isle of MTV and the 10th anniversary of the Glitch Festival are turning the islands into a global hub for electronic music. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Med Minds 06 Jean de Valette
programme.desc: Jean Parisot de Valette, the steadfast Grand Master of Malta, led the Knights with courage, defended his island, and saved Europe as we know it.

programme.title: Whats On Lights, Cinema, Mediterranean
programme.desc: The Mediterranean screens its stories this week. Malta hosts the Mediterranean Film Festival, while Italy Venice Film Festival and Med Film Festival bring glamour and regional storytelling to iconic locations. France Cannes Film Festival shines globally, Spain International Fantastic Film Festival of Catalonia celebrates genre cinema, and Morocco Amazigh Film Festival showcases North African voices. Turkey Adana Golden Boll Film Festival and Croatia Zagreb Film Festival round out a cinematic journey across the Med.

programme.desc: Fire Under The Sea

programme.title: Sapiens: Mediterraneo Arabo Part 1
programme.desc: Sapiens: Mediterraneo Arabo Part 1

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Here along the Côte d’Azur, we wander the sunlit promenades of Nice, catch whispers of fortunes changing hands at Monaco’s famed casino, and step inside the elegant villas once home to artists and heiresses.

programme.title: Whats On Trade, Exhibitions and Unique Experiences
programme.desc: There is a unique energy that takes over the Mediterranean as the summer heat begins to build. This week, we are bypassing the usual tourist routes to find the heartbeat of the region through its most spectacular festivals. Imagine the sun setting over a lake in Tuscany as the Puccini Festival opens its 72nd season with the powerful melodies of Turandot. From that timeless elegance, we head straight into the high energy of Malta, where the Isle of MTV and the 10th anniversary of the Glitch Festival are turning the islands into a global hub for electronic music. We also explore the stunning heights of the Spanish Pyrenees for the floating stage at Pirineos Sur and hit the iconic cliffs of Santorini for the urban vibes of the Supa Dupa Fly Festival. This is a journey through a region that knows exactly how to celebrate its past while dancing firmly into the future.

programme.title: Brač (Supetar)
programme.desc: On Brač, famed for its white stone, Zlatni Rat beach, and Vidova Gora, we stopped in Supetar to taste tradition. Restaurateur Teo Dragičević served us šporka juha, mišanca, and vitalac—authentic island flavors rooted in Brač’s rich culinary heritage.

programme.title: Med Destinations Lake Bled

programme.title: MED DST MAKARSKA 15MIN
programme.desc: WITH VO

programme.title: Gold Cuisine EP 4
programme.desc: Chryso Lefou makes a light and tasty Mediterranean dish with fresh pasta, colourful mint pesto, and juicy cherry tomatoes. She makes crispy fried almond fingers (Dactyla) for dessert, which adds a nutty, sweet finish. A delectable blend of decadent treats and savoury, fresh flavours.

programme.desc: MEMOIRS JUNE 20

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature EP8
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Med Nations 06 Spain
programme.desc: Explore Spain, a country where sun-drenched beaches converge with vibrant cities steeped in centuries of history. From the artistic avenues of Barcelona to the lively Mediterranean ports, Spain presents a dynamic blend of culture, cuisine, and coastal traditions that embody its distinctive Mediterranean spirit.

programme.title: Med Minds 06 Jean de Valette
programme.desc: Jean Parisot de Valette, the steadfast Grand Master of Malta, led the Knights with courage, defended his island, and saved Europe as we know it.

programme.title: Whats On Lights, Cinema, Mediterranean
programme.desc: The Mediterranean screens its stories this week. Malta hosts the Mediterranean Film Festival, while Italy Venice Film Festival and Med Film Festival bring glamour and regional storytelling to iconic locations. France Cannes Film Festival shines globally, Spain International Fantastic Film Festival of Catalonia celebrates genre cinema, and Morocco Amazigh Film Festival showcases North African voices. Turkey Adana Golden Boll Film Festival and Croatia Zagreb Film Festival round out a cinematic journey across the Med.

programme.title: MEMOIRS JUNE 20
programme.desc: MEMOIRS JUNE 20

programme.title: Overland Da Malta alla Turchia l altro capo del Mediterraneo
programme.desc: Journey on a captivating trek across Gozo, Malta, exploring its rich history, from Ulysses’ legendary journey to resistance against Ottoman invasions. We discover local wine cultures, prehistoric ruins, and the island’s transformation under Napoleon. The adventure continues to Turkey, where we trace ancient civilisations like the Hittites, Romans, and Greeks, following the path of Alexander the Great.

programme.title: Med Destinations Crete
programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Egypt Yesterday and Today
programme.desc: In this hour-long special, Rick Steves explores the historic and cultural wonders of Egypt, from Cairo and Alexandria to Luxor and Abu Simbel.

programme.title: The Great Greek Myths
programme.desc: Athena: Armed Wisdom reveals the story of the goddess who combines intellect with strength, emerging fully grown from the head of Zeus. As the goddess of wisdom and warfare, she guides heroes and protects cities with her strategic mind. Athena's power lies not in brute force, but in her ability to outthink and outmaneuver her foes. A myth that highlights the harmony between wisdom and strength.

programme.title: Madame Butterfly Teatro Alla Scala 2016
programme.desc: In Madama Butterfly (Teatro alla Scala, 2016), a young Japanese woman enters into a marriage with an American naval officer, filled with hope for a new life. As time passes, the contrast between her expectations and reality becomes more profound. The opera unfolds with deep emotional intensity, leading to a powerful and poignant conclusion.

programme.title: Concerto – OSN Gatti Bruckner Wagner Torino
programme.desc: In January 2022, Daniele Gatti conducted the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai at the Turin Auditorium, performing Wagner’s "Parsifal" excerpts and Bruckner’s Symphony No. 9. The programme highlighted Wagner's meditative Prelude to Act III and Good Friday Spell, followed by Bruckner’s profound Ninth Symphony, marked by its emotional depth and unfinished final movement.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Join Rick as we explore Andalucía - prancing horses, whitewashed hilltowns, kite surfing on sunny beaches. Then we'll summit the Rock of Gibraltar and sample Morocco, from tribal musicians and busy artisans to the magic of its markets

programme.title: Malini Angelica Athens
programme.desc: Malini explores the ancient city of Athens, Greece. Once steeped in history, Athens has experienced a modern rebirth, now thriving with creativity, modern Greek gastronomy, a vibrant contemporary art scene, and unmatched nightlife.

programme.title: The Gypsies S1
programme.desc: Driving through ancient cities, holy river towns, national parks and hills in Madhya Pradesh, Krisann and Benafsha finally reach its capital, Bhopal.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: The medieval treasury of Czech kings, Kutná Hora owes its prominence to its mines. In fact, the richness of the city’s silver mines was what saw it declared a royal city and made the seat of the Bohemian Royal Mint. As Rohan visits the city’s significant monuments such as a cathedral dedicated to the patron saint of all miners and the erstwhile central mint of the country, he finds out interesting stories about its mining and minting history.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: From the world's largest shopping mall to a magical desert safari, Sabrina realizes that Dubai is one of the most glamorous travel destinations in the world.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.desc: A stunning contrast of deserts, mountains and beaches, its unspoilt landscape, dotted with grand forts, exquisite palaces, undulating dunes, canyons and lush wadis.

programme.title: Landmarks
programme.desc: Commissioned by William the Conqueror, York Castle in England has served as a center for regional governance, a royal mint, and a notorious prison. From the martyrdom of a famous English saint to the most gruesome Jewish pogrom in British history, the castle has witnessed nine centuries of tumultuous history and has many stories to tell its visitors.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Cologne is all things romantic from the chocolate museum to its perfume shop. It is a city in love with life, one that knows how to have a good time.

programme.title: The Gypsies S1
programme.desc: Two adventurous girls embark on a road trip across Madhya Pradesh, the heart of India, and their first stop is Mandu, a town of magnificent palaces.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Renowned journalist and traveler, Emeline Nsingi Nkosi visits the colourful, clean and minimalist seaside city of Helsinki, the capital of Finland. Here, she explores the iconic attractions, right from magnificent historical fortresses and monuments to churches and libraries with groundbreaking design. She finds out that Helsinki offers a fascinating journey through centuries of evolving architecture and style. She also pampers herself at a Finnish sauna.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Heather in Tenerife explores colonial buildings and later visits Santa Cruz de Tenerife, ending her day at San Andreas. Next day explores a zoological park and visits 1000+ year old Tree.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: Kim Jagtiani visits the pint-sized Maldivian capital, Malé, and finds out that its bustle is in stark contrast to the secluded luxury of the island resorts for which the archipelago is known. She goes on a tour to see some of the iconic attractions of Malé such as the Islamic centre, the President’s house and the National Museum, which houses ancient artefacts and royal antiquities. Along the way, the rich history and culture of the Maldives, often overlooked by tourists, unfolds before her.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: In Addis Ababa, Emeline visits Lucy and Ardi, the ancestors of mankind, and tries Ethiopian injera. Next, she visits Bahir Dar and Lake Tana.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: What can Udaipur offer you on a quick getaway? Luxury? Romance? A chance to go back in time? Is that all? Asmita goes on a short break to Udaipur to find out the best things to do in Rajasthan’s most glamorous city. From uncovering sagas of Rajput bravery at the memorials built for the Rajputs who ruled the city to taking a horseback tour in Udaipur’s lesser-known countryside, she discovers that this is a city waiting to be explored.

programme.title: The Gypsies S1
programme.desc: On a road trip across central India, Krisann and Benafsha stumble upon Maheshwar, a town where the legacy of an 18th century queen comes alive.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Santa Claus has an official residence right by the Arctic Circle, where the Christmas spirit can be felt all round the year. Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him, and gets a few tips from the man himself on how to make it to the nice list. She also tries out the surreal and utterly relaxing experience of floating on a lake while gazing up at the midnight sun.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Heather tries Paella, Los Gigantes village and also the Pyramids of Guimar. She also enjoys stargazing atop El Teide and tries jet skiing at Playa del Duque, ending at Siam water park.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: Elisha Kriis gets the ultimate experience of land and marine wildlife at Safari World and Siam Ocean World in Bangkok, the capital of Thailand.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: In Gondar, Emeline visits the historical Gondar castle, plays with adorable Gelada baboons, and sees the awe-inspiring rock-hewn Lalibela churches.

programme.title: The Gypsies S1
programme.desc: Built on the backwaters of Indira Sagar Dam, the island of Hanuwantiya was designed to be a paradise for adventure lovers.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Heather visits Casa De Colon museum, a rum cellar and then Puerto de Mogan. Later explores Maspalomas Dunes and wraps up after trying chestnut mousse.

programme.title: Xp Guide
programme.desc: An intoxicating mix of idyllic beaches, wild adventures and a vibrant nightlife, the island of Boracay in the Philippines makes Elisha Kriis addicted.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: One of Uzbekistan’s most ancient cities, Bukhara was a popular town even during the days of the Silk Route trading. Built atop a constellation of small oases here weary desert travelers could find water and rest. Through its madrassahs, minarets, a massive royal fortress and the remnants of a once-vast market complex, Alex Outhwaite soaks in the vibes of the Central Asia of yore.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Finland is a popular destination in the winter, but Emeline Nsingi Nkosi finds out what are the things to enjoy here in the summer. She goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies. She learns about the water spirit of Finland when she goes fishing with a local. She also participates in a curious Finnish tradition called New Year’s Tin.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: Emeline visits Axum, the land of the Queen of Sheba and the Lost Ark of the Covenant. Next, she tries the coffee ceremony and hyena feeding in Harar.

programme.desc: Built on the backwaters of Indira Sagar Dam, the island of Hanuwantiya was designed to be a paradise for adventure lovers.

programme.title: Off The Grid
programme.desc: Emeline visits Axum, the land of the Queen of Sheba and the Lost Ark of the Covenant. Next, she tries the coffee ceremony and hyena feeding in Harar.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: Stockholm, the capital and largest city of Sweden, was built on 14 Islands. This place is also sometimes referred as Venice of the north.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: This exhilarating city of castles, beer and segway rides can take you on a break that will truly leave you catching your breath.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Visit Belgrade, with Heather as she explores the city, its architecture and learns about its past by visiting historical monuments.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.
